summary: Active external reports were reviewed against current code. Closed or superseded reports were archived, the 2026-05-22 self-improvement report is archived, and explicit strategy actions now remain machine-visible through the action matrix instead of being hidden behind broad implemented action IDs.

observations.problem: A new top-level active report, external-reports/LLMwiki_lane_separation_revalidation_report_20260522.md, exists after the checked-in reference manifest was generated, so the checked-in manifest and action matrix omit it even though recomputation detects reference_manifest_alignment_status=drift.
observations.why_it_matters: Active external reports are operator-facing evidence. If manifest and action matrix artifacts can remain stale after a new root report lands, closeout can claim complete active report coverage while missing the newest P0/P1 recommendations.
observations.automation_candidate: Add a cheap active-report-lifecycle freshness check to closeout/static paths that compares root external-reports files against the manifest and action matrix without rewriting canonical artifacts, and route repair through make external-report-lifecycle-refresh.
observations.suggested_followup: Closed in this task. The reference manifest and action matrix are current for all root active external reports, tests/test_external_report_lifecycle_static.py guards checked-in manifest/matrix drift, and marker-wide report-contract/release checks now include that guard through the *-all lanes.

observations.problem: release evidence closeout previously combined canonical report writers, fixed-point convergence, tmp cleanup, and finality verification in one ambiguous target chain; the canonical mutating lane is now release-evidence-converge, with release-verify-current and release-sealed-verify as check-only lanes and release-evidence-closeout retained only as a compatibility alias.
observations.why_it_matters: A lane that both repairs currentness and asserts release authority makes dirty state, fixed-point repair, and sealed authority harder to reason about and keeps the operator-facing closeout vocabulary ambiguous.
observations.automation_candidate: Keep Makefile/static tests, execution-lane policy, workflow planner recommendations, order guard checks, and signoff defaults pinned to release-evidence-converge/current/sealed names.
observations.suggested_followup: Implemented release-evidence-converge, release-verify-current, and release-sealed-verify as canonical lanes; migrated planner/order-guard/signoff/docs/tests to the new names. Next remove the compatibility alias after external CI/release callers no longer depend on release-evidence-closeout.

observations.surface: ops/scripts/release/release_closeout_summary.py, ops/schemas/release-closeout-summary.schema.json, release status v2 consumers, release lane/operator summaries
observations.problem: release-closeout-summary still emits top-level sealed_release_status and status_v2.status_axes.sealed_release_status for a pre-distribution/source closeout surface, while batch/operator sealed surfaces can simultaneously report sealed_clean_pass on a bound ZIP basis.
observations.why_it_matters: The same field name appears at different authority levels, so a clean source closeout with no distribution ZIP can look inconsistent with sealed batch/operator evidence even when both are individually correct.
observations.automation_candidate: Add pre_distribution_package_binding_status and source_closeout_distribution_binding_status as first-class schema fields, keep sealed_release_status as a deprecated compatibility alias for one migration window, and update consumers/tests to prefer the lower-authority names on summary surfaces.
observations.suggested_followup: Implemented pre_distribution_package_binding_status and source_closeout_distribution_binding_status as first-class closeout-summary fields and status_v2 axes; sealed_release_status remains only as a deprecated compatibility alias for the migration window.

observations.surface: mk/test.mk, ops/test-lane-registry.json, tests/test_makefile_static_gates.py, release_sealing/report_contract pytest lanes
observations.problem: test-release-sealing still runs an explicit 39-test file set while release_sealing marks 46 tests, and test-report-contract runs a 191-test core pack while the report_contract marker-wide set contains 411 tests.
observations.why_it_matters: The explicit selector packs may be valid core lanes, but names that read like marker-wide lanes make coverage expectations easy to overstate and hide drift unless core/all parity is machine-checked.
observations.automation_candidate: Rename or alias explicit packs to *-core, add marker-wide *-all targets, and add parity guards that require intentional registry entries for any marker-covered file excluded from the core pack.
observations.suggested_followup: Implemented explicit *-core targets and marker-wide *-all targets for report_contract and release_sealing, migrated CI/release-check entrypoints to the all lanes, and retained old names only as compatibility aliases.

observations.problem: The active 2026-05-22 lane separation report maps only to broad existing action IDs; it does not create distinct matrix actions for lane mutability split, sealed vocabulary demotion, selector/marker parity, or active manifest freshness.
observations.why_it_matters: Without explicit action IDs, the matrix can show most actions as implemented while the newest report's actual P0 recommendations remain only in prose.
observations.automation_candidate: Extend ACTION_CATALOG with dedicated lane-separation, sealed-summary-vocabulary, selector-marker-parity, and active-report-manifest-freshness actions, backed by focused evidence predicates.
observations.suggested_followup: Implemented dedicated action IDs and tests for active manifest freshness, release lane mutability split, sealed summary vocabulary demotion, and selector/marker parity; continue using planned/partial statuses as the remaining implementation roadmap.

observations.problem: The active self-improvement reports carried unresolved strategy themes such as artifact freshness observability, repo boundary hygiene, GitHub-native security automation, generated artifact tracking policy, public export negative assertions, supply-chain external verification, and collaboration governance, but the previous matrix could still show every action as implemented.
observations.why_it_matters: Archive decisions depend on the matrix. If unresolved strategy themes are not modeled as distinct actions, an active report can be archived merely because nearby lower-level evidence exists.
observations.automation_candidate: Extend the external report action catalog with explicit self-improvement strategy actions and status predicates that stay partially_automated until the canonical evidence proves the full recommendation.
observations.suggested_followup: Closed in this task. Added eight dedicated self-improvement strategy action IDs and then implemented the directly connected items for artifact freshness observability, public export negative assertions, GitHub-native security automation, collaboration governance, generated artifact tracking policy, and archived-run namespace cleanup. The active 2026-05-22 self-improvement report remains active with only the larger repo-boundary, maintainability-hotspot, and external supply-chain verification themes still partial.

observations.problem: After external-report lifecycle cleanup, generated-artifact-index still reports one non-external archive candidate: a run whose promotion-report history is archived but whose run directory remains in the top-level runs namespace.
observations.why_it_matters: Leaving unrelated archive candidates open can make future lifecycle closeout look incomplete even when the active external-report set is current.
observations.automation_candidate: Route archived run namespace cleanup through the existing archive-execution-manifest path or add a run-specific relocation guard that preserves aliases and rollback evidence.
observations.suggested_followup: Closed in this task through make archive-execution-manifest followed by archive-execution-manifest-apply with operator confirmation. The archived run directory moved under runs/archive/ and generated-artifact-index now reports archive_candidate_count=0.

observations.problem: After the directly connected fixes in this task, three active-report strategy themes remained partially automated: physical public/private repo boundary and history absence evidence, maintainability hotspot decomposition, and external supply-chain verification beyond local integrity checks.
observations.why_it_matters: These items were action-matrix attention items, but each required concrete repo-boundary, maintainability, or supply-chain evidence rather than prose-only acceptance.
observations.automation_candidate: Keep repo-boundary history absence, maintainability refactor backlog, and external supply-chain verification as explicit matrix predicates whose status can only pass from canonical evidence.
observations.suggested_followup: Closed in this task. public-check-summary now records materialized export split and private-history absence evidence; function-budget refactor proposals provide canonical hotspot decomposition backlog evidence; sigstore/dependency-review/provenance wiring is detected as external verification readiness without overclaiming a live external bundle. external-report-action-matrix now reports implemented_count=36 and partially_automated_count=0, and the active self-improvement report has been archived.

observations.problem: Codex native goal state is useful for coordination but does not by itself preserve checkpoint evidence, resume commands, workspace locks, or promotion blockers across long-running repository work.
observations.why_it_matters: A long auto-improve or release-maintenance goal can look active in the assistant thread while durable repo evidence is stale, incomplete, or tied to a different contract digest.
observations.automation_candidate: Keep native goal state advisory and require file-backed goal contracts, goal-run-status, checkpoint/resume metadata, workspace lock evidence, and runtime certificate checks before completion or promotion claims.
observations.suggested_followup: Repo-side guard is automated: FileGoalBackend/run-local file contracts are process-persistent, fake backends require explicit allow_fake, update_goal revalidates the merged contract, and goal-runtime-run-admission now blocks promotion authority unless file-backed contract, goal-run-status, and runtime-certificate evidence agree on the verified contract digest. The native Codex thread goal remains advisory and cannot itself be made durable from repository code.

observations.problem: This task ran a full pytest pass before public-check-summary and generated-artifact convergence, then had to run another full pytest after those canonical writers changed checked-in report fingerprints.
observations.why_it_matters: The evidence was ultimately correct, but the ordering spent an avoidable extra full-suite pass and made closeout feel less deterministic. Public-check-summary is not just a check: it promotes a canonical report, so it belongs before the final full-suite evidence boundary.
observations.automation_candidate: Codify a validation DAG for goal closeout: run static checks, policy/public/check summary writers, report-schema/public/generated-artifact convergence, and other canonical writers first; then run one final full pytest; after that, allow only check-only or narrowly targeted schema/report-contract validation unless source files change again.
observations.suggested_followup: Closed in this task. The goal prompt and README closeout guidance classify generated-artifact convergence, public-check-summary, report schema samples, release smoke reuse, and artifact finalization as ordered closeout surfaces; tests/test_codex_goal_prompt.py asserts that this guidance remains present in the prompt, README.md, and ops/README.md.

observations.problem: During this task, individually refreshing report-schema samples, release-smoke, test-execution-summary, dashboard, closeout summary, and external-report-action-matrix repeatedly invalidated the finality digest map until release-closeout-fixed-point was rerun as the terminal writer.
observations.why_it_matters: The finality model is sound, but operators doing narrow report repairs can accidentally create several write-then-reseal cycles before discovering that finality must be terminal. That increases closeout latency and makes otherwise successful repairs look unstable.
observations.automation_candidate: Add a cheap resettle planner or focused Make wrapper that detects touched generated-report families, runs only the necessary post-writer generated-artifact convergence, then terminates with release-closeout-fixed-point and finality verify. Keep finality verify check-only and terminal.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated in this task. Added make release-finality-resettle as the focused wrapper for narrow generated-report/observation repairs, taught workflow-dependency-planner to recommend that lane for canonical report and improvement-observation changes, and added release-workflow-order-guard/static tests that require release-closeout-fixed-point before terminal release-closeout-finality-verify.
